user_story.i_want: complete testing infrastructure with 100% coverage enforcement
user_story.so_that: every feature I build has automated tests preventing regressions

criterion.description: Vitest Configuration - vite.config.ts includes Vitest setup with coverage provider (c8/v8), coverage thresholds enforced: 100% (statements, branches, functions, lines), browser mode disabled initially, test command: npm run test runs all unit/integration tests, UI mode: npm run test:ui launches Vitest UI

React Testing Library - @testing-library/react@16.3.0 and @testing-library/dom installed, custom render function in tests/test-utils.tsx wraps components with providers, example test: src/shared/components/StreakCounter.test.tsx (create placeholder component first), screen reader queries work: getByRole, getByLabelText, getByText

Playwright E2E - playwright.config.ts configured for Chromium, Firefox, WebKit, mobile viewport: 375×667 (iPhone SE), base URL: http://localhost:5173, test command: npm run test:e2e runs E2E tests, trace on first retry enabled for debugging
CI script runs: npm run test && npm run test:e2e && npm run build
Coverage report generated in coverage/ directory (HTML + lcov)
Example test passes for all three frameworks

tasks.task: Configure Vitest with 100% coverage thresholds in vite.config.ts
Install and configure React Testing Library with custom render wrapper
Create placeholder StreakCounter component and example test
Configure Playwright for E2E tests (Chromium, Firefox, WebKit)
Add test scripts to package.json (test, test:ui, test:e2e)
Create example E2E test that passes
Test complete CI pipeline: test → E2E → build

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Story 1.6 establishes comprehensive testing infrastructure: Vitest for unit/integration tests (fast, Vite-native), React Testing Library for component tests (user-centric queries), Playwright for E2E tests (Chromium, Firefox, WebKit; mobile viewport 375×667), 100% coverage threshold enforced in CI (c8 coverage provider), Co-located test files (Component.test.tsx next to Component.tsx), AAA pattern mandatory (Arrange, Act, Assert)

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Epic 1 establishes a test-first, coverage-enforced culture where every line of code must be covered by automated tests. Core Principles: 100% Coverage Mandatory, AAA Pattern, Co-Located Tests, Test Pyramid (many unit tests, fewer integration tests, minimal E2E tests), Accessibility First (test with screen reader queries)

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Vitest configuration enforces 100% coverage thresholds for statements, branches, functions, and lines. Coverage reporter: text, html, lcov. Exclusions: tests/**, **/*.test.tsx, **/*.config.ts, **/index.ts (re-export files). CI enforcement: Build fails if any coverage threshold < 100%, coverage report uploaded as GitHub artifact, PR comments show coverage diff, merge blocked until all tests pass.

doc.title: Architecture Specification
doc.snippet: Vitest for unit/integration tests (fast, Vite-native), React Testing Library for component tests (user-centric queries), Playwright for E2E tests (Chromium, Firefox, WebKit; mobile viewport 375×667), 100% coverage threshold enforced in CI (c8 coverage provider), Co-located test files (Component.test.tsx next to Component.tsx), AAA pattern mandatory (Arrange, Act, Assert)

doc.title: Architecture Specification
doc.snippet: Co-located test files, AAA pattern (Arrange, Act, Assert), 100% coverage required

artifact.reason: PARTIALLY IMPLEMENTED: Vitest configuration already exists with 100% coverage thresholds (statements, branches, functions, lines), v8 coverage provider, jsdom environment, vitest.setup.ts setupFiles, and exclusions configured. This is the main config file to verify/enhance for Story 1.6.
ALREADY IMPLEMENTED: Vitest setup file configuring fake-indexeddb and @testing-library/jest-dom matchers. This provides the test environment needed for Story 1.6.
EXISTING TEST: Example unit test demonstrating Vitest test structure and AAA pattern. Can be used as reference for writing new tests.
EXISTING TEST: Example unit test validating module exports. Demonstrates test organization and assertions.
EXISTING TEST: Example unit test validating path alias configuration. Shows how to test configuration correctness.
EXISTING TEST: Integration test demonstrating Dexie database testing with fake-indexeddb. Shows integration test patterns for database operations.
EXISTING TEST: React component test using React Testing Library. Demonstrates component testing patterns, context testing, and RTL queries (getByRole, getByText, etc.).
EXISTING TEST: React component test demonstrating context provider testing patterns with RTL.
EXISTING TEST: React component test showing localStorage integration testing patterns.
EXISTING TEST: Dexie database service test demonstrating service layer testing patterns.
EXISTING TEST: LocalStorage service test showing storage testing patterns with mocking.

interface.name: Vitest Configuration
interface.signature: test: { pool: 'forks', environment: 'jsdom', globals: true, passWithNoTests: true, setupFiles: ['./vitest.setup.ts'], coverage: { provider: 'v8', reporter: ['text', 'html', 'lcov'], thresholds: { statements: 100, branches: 100, functions: 100, lines: 100 }, exclude: [...] } }

constraints.constraint: MANDATORY: 100% test coverage threshold enforced (statements, branches, functions, lines)
MANDATORY: Co-locate test files next to source files (Component.test.tsx next to Component.tsx)
MANDATORY: Follow AAA pattern (Arrange, Act, Assert) in all tests
MANDATORY: Use React Testing Library user-centric queries (getByRole, getByLabelText, getByText) over implementation details
MANDATORY: Configure Playwright for mobile viewport 375×667 (iPhone SE)
MANDATORY: Coverage reports generated in HTML and lcov formats
Test execution: Unit/integration tests must run in <10 seconds
E2E tests should cover critical paths only (not exhaustive)
Exclude test files, config files, and index.ts re-exports from coverage
Use fake-indexeddb for Dexie database testing (already configured in vitest.setup.ts)
Playwright base URL: http://localhost:5173 (Vite dev server)
Enable trace on first retry for debugging E2E failures

tests.standards: Epic 1 establishes test-first, coverage-enforced culture. All code requires automated tests with 100% coverage. Testing frameworks: Vitest 4.0 (unit/integration), React Testing Library 16.3.0 (component tests), Playwright 1.56.1 (E2E tests). Test files co-located with source. AAA pattern mandatory. Accessibility-first with screen reader queries. Test pyramid: many unit tests, fewer integration tests, minimal E2E tests. Test environment: jsdom with fake-indexeddb support.

ideas.idea: Unit test: Verify vite.config.ts contains test configuration with coverage thresholds set to 100% for all metrics. Verify coverage provider is 'v8'. Verify setupFiles includes vitest.setup.ts.

Integration test: Create tests/test-utils.tsx with custom render function that wraps components with all context providers (UserSettingsProvider, AppProvider, SessionProvider). Verify it works by creating a simple test component that uses contexts.

Component test: Create placeholder StreakCounter component (if not exists) and StreakCounter.test.tsx demonstrating RTL queries (getByRole, getByText, getByLabelText). Test should render component with props and verify output.

Config test: Verify playwright.config.ts exists and contains configuration for Chromium, Firefox, WebKit browsers. Verify mobile viewport 375×667. Verify base URL http://localhost:5173. Verify trace on first retry enabled.
Integration test: Create script or test that runs npm run test && npm run test:e2e && npm run build sequentially and verifies all commands exit with code 0.
Unit test: After running coverage, verify coverage/ directory exists and contains index.html and lcov.info files. Verify lcov.info is parseable.

E2E test: Create simple Playwright test (e.g., tests/e2e/smoke.spec.ts) that navigates to homepage, verifies page loaded, clicks a navigation element, verifies route change. Should pass on all three browsers.
